answer:
\boxed{C}

The **predominant III in C minor (B♭-D-F)** is enharmonically equivalent to **A♭ major (A♭-C-E)**, which is the **subdominant in A♭ minor (6-4)** (①). The **dominant seventh in A minor (A-C-E♭-G)** is enharmonic to **G♯-minor 7 (G♯-B-D-F♯)**, not the same chord (②). The **predominant III in E minor (B-D-F)** is enharmonic to **C major (C-E-G)**, not the same (③). The **dominant #5-6 in E major (B-D-F♯-A)** is enharmonic to **A♭-minor 7♭5 (A♭-C-E♭-G♭)**, not the same (④). Only **①
